title: Electric Three-Wheeler Sales Surge by 16% in Mumbai Amid High CNG Prices
author: Preeti Sharma
summary: Electric Three-Wheeler Sales Surge by 16% in Mumbai Amid High CNG Prices. This comprehensive industry update details the market movements, sales metrics, and commercial impacts across India.
content:
Electric three-wheelers are experiencing unprecedented demand in Mumbai as operating costs for CNG autos climb. Logistics fleet operators are switching over 60% of their new acquisitions to commercial EVs. Leading makers like Mahindra Electric, Altigreen, and Piaggio have reported waiting periods of up to 4 weeks. Fleet owners are citing a substantial increase in monthly savings.

According to industry analysts, this trend will continue to shape the micro-logistics environment. The cost comparison shows that EVs operate at just ₹0.80 per km, compared to CNG at ₹2.20 per km and Diesel at ₹3.80 per km. Dealership networks are adding robust charging docks and fast-swapping battery options to handle commercial logistics fleet uptimes. Major retail and e-commerce companies are mandating 100% EV utilization for urban deliveries by next year.

title: Government Announces FAME III Guidelines with Dedicated Incentives for Cargo Three-Wheelers
author: Rohan Mehta
summary: Government Announces FAME III Guidelines with Dedicated Incentives for Cargo Three-Wheelers. This comprehensive industry update details the market movements, sales metrics, and commercial impacts across India.
content:
The Ministry of Heavy Industries has unveiled the draft guidelines for the FAME III subsidy program. Under the new program, electric three-wheelers (both passenger and cargo loaders) will qualify for direct subsidies ranging from ₹25,000 to ₹50,000 depending on battery size. This policy is set to accelerate last-mile delivery transitions to clean fuels over the next three fiscal years.

According to industry analysts, this trend will continue to shape the micro-logistics environment. The cost comparison shows that EVs operate at just ₹0.80 per km, compared to CNG at ₹2.20 per km and Diesel at ₹3.80 per km. Dealership networks are adding robust charging docks and fast-swapping battery options to handle commercial logistics fleet uptimes. Major retail and e-commerce companies are mandating 100% EV utilization for urban deliveries by next year.
